skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars. Triggers: video generation, ai video, text to video, image to video, veo, animate image, video from image, ai animation, video generator, generate video, t2v, i2v, ai video maker, create video with ai, runway alternative, pika alternative, sora alternative, kling alternative
skill.summary: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: All 62 static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation file for AI video generation via inference.sh CLI. The 'external_commands' detections are markdown code blocks (backticks for CLI syntax), not actual code execution. The 'network' detections are legitimate documentation URLs and example URLs. No cryptographic code exists. No prompt injection attempts detected.

high_findings.title: External Commands Detection - False Positive
high_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at 44 locations. These are markdown code fences (```bash) used to display CLI commands, not actual code execution. The skill is documentation-only.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is pure Markdown documentation. Backticks are used for code block syntax in markdown, not Ruby execution. No Ruby or shell scripts exist in this file.

high_findings.title: Network Access Detection - False Positive
high_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 16 'Hardcoded URL' patterns. These are legitimate documentation URLs (inference.sh website) and example URLs for image/video inputs in documentation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are documentation links and example placeholders (e.g., 'https://your-image.jpg'). No data exfiltration detected.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3 and 59. No cryptographic code exists in this Markdown documentation file.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML frontmatter description. Line 59 is a markdown table row. No cryptographic algorithms present.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos with Google Veo and More
content.value_statement: Creating videos manually is time-consuming and expensive. This skill provides access to 40+ AI video generation models through a simple CLI interface, enabling rapid video creation for social media, marketing, and content creation.
actual_capabilities: Generate text-to-video using Google Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance, and Grok models
Create image-to-video animations from static images using Wan 2.5
Produce AI avatar videos with lipsync from images and audio files
Upscale video quality and add foley sound effects
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account
Video generation depends on external API availability and rate limits
Quality varies by model; some models require paid API credits
Output is subject to AI model capabilities and limitations

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creator
use_cases.description: Quickly generate engaging video content for social media platforms with AI-powered visuals and transitions
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Marketing Professional
use_cases.description: Create product demos and marketing videos without expensive production equipment
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and small business owners

use_cases.title: Content Producer
use_cases.description: Produce explainer videos and educational content with AI avatars and voice synchronization

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video using Veo 3.1 Fast with the prompt: [YOUR PROMPT DESCRIPTION]

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-Video Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate my image at [IMAGE_URL] into a video using Wan 2.5

prompt_templates.title: AI Avatar with LipSync
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an AI avatar video using the image [IMAGE_URL] and audio [AUDIO_URL] with OmniHuman 1.5

prompt_templates.title: Video with Sound Effects
prompt_templates.prompt: Add foley sound effects to [VIDEO_URL] with the prompt: [SOUND DESCRIPTION]

output_examples.input: Generate a drone shot video of a forest
output_examples.output: Returns a video file generated by Veo 3.1 Fast showing an aerial view of a forest

output_examples.input: Animate my portrait into a talking head video
output_examples.output: Creates an AI avatar that speaks using the provided audio file

output_examples.input: Upscale my video to higher resolution
output_examples.output: Returns an enhanced quality version of the input video

best_practices: Use descriptive, specific prompts for better video quality
Choose the right model for your use case: Veo for quality, Grok for speed
Preview with shorter durations first before generating full videos
anti_patterns: Using overly complex prompts that confuse the AI model
Ignoring model-specific requirements (some need specific image/audio formats)
Not checking API credits before large batch generations

faq.question: Do I need an inference.sh account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to sign up at inference.sh and configure your API credentials to use this skill.

faq.question: Which model is best for text-to-video?
faq.answer: Google Veo 3.1 offers the best quality. Veo 3.1 Fast is quicker for prototyping.

faq.question: Can I use my own images for video generation?
faq.answer: Yes, image-to-video models like Wan 2.5 and Seedance accept custom image URLs.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Generation time varies by model and length, typically 1-5 minutes for short videos.

faq.question: Is there a free tier available?
faq.answer: Inference.sh offers API credits; check their pricing page for current options.

faq.question: Can I add voiceovers to AI-generated videos?
faq.answer: Yes, use lipsync tools like OmniHuman or Fabric with your audio file.
